title: Contract C01: Project → Research
description: Governs the transition from Stage 0 (Project Setup) to Stage 1 (Research). Defines what project.json must contain before research begins, what research.json must produce at minimum, and how failures are handled.

input.description: Fields from project.json that Stage 1 (Research) requires before it can begin.

required_fields.purpose: Used as the primary identifier for all research documents and log entries.
The subject of research. Research agent generates facts and sources about this topic.
Determines language of narration facts and source reliability expectations.
Used to calculate minimum fact density. A 12-min video needs deeper research than a 5-min video.
dark_documentary style → research must include historical context, human stories, dispute disclosures.
Niche drives template selection (e.g. japanese_mystery → japan_template.md) which sets required beats.

optional_fields.purpose: If present, research agent uses it to calibrate fact count. Calculated as duration_minutes × 130 × language_multiplier.

preconditions: project.topic must not be empty or null

output.description: What research.json must contain when Stage 1 delivers its output to Stage 2.

required_fields.purpose: Each fact must have a fact_id (VF[0-9]+), claim, status, and at least one source_id.
Each source must have a source_id (S[0-9]+), title, and reliability enum.
PASS only when fail_count = 0 and flag adjustments are complete. Stage 2 (source_verification) sets this field.
Count of facts with status=flag. Language adjustments required for each.
Must be 0 after Stage 2 adjustment pass. Any fail_count > 0 halts the pipeline.
False if fail_count > 0 or if a critical rule violation exists. Stage 3 must not start if false.

constraints: All fact_id values must match pattern ^VF[0-9]{1,3}$
All source_id values must match pattern ^S[0-9]{1,3}$
Facts with status=fail may not appear in story.json. pipeline_continue must be false.
Facts with status=flag must have language adjusted before leaving Stage 2. Adjusted claim stored in adjusted_claim field.
No fabricated URLs in any source.url field (MASTER_RULE Rule 2)
Disputed facts must have both sides of dispute described in claim or notes

checks.description: facts array has at least 5 items
checks.action_on_fail: Halt pipeline. Return to Stage 1 with instruction to expand research.

checks.description: sources array has at least 3 items
All FLAG items have adjusted_claim populated
No source has a fabricated or non-verifiable URL
checks.action_on_fail: Mark source as unverified. Remove URL field.

checks.description: Each fact references at least one valid source_id
checks.action_on_fail: Mark fact as unverified. Flag for human review.

failure_conditions.human_action: Review flagged facts. Either find verifiable replacement claims or delete the facts.
Re-run Stage 1 with expanded research prompt.
Add at least one high or medium reliability source before proceeding.

recovery_strategy.strategy: For each fact with status=fail: (1) attempt to find verifiable replacement claim from a different source; (2) if not found, delete the fact; (3) if fact is load-bearing (referenced by outline section), replace with a weaker, verified claim; (4) re-run Stage 2 verification.
Apply language softening to adjusted_claim: superlatives → qualifiers; absolute statements → probabilistic statements. Do not change meaning. Update the fact.claim field.
Re-run Stage 1 with explicit instruction to expand research scope. Add secondary search vectors: academic papers, government records, UNESCO documents.

valid_output_summary.note: NEEDS_REVISION because 3 FLAG items require language adjustments. pipeline_continue=true because fail_count=0 and adjustments are pending (not failed).
